title: TaskManager::update_task uses O(N) linear scans inside a 150-line god function
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/session/task.rs:185-341
## Violation
update_task is annotated allow(clippy::too_many_lines) and has cyclomatic complexity well above 15: it handles status transitions (including Delete), validates dependency references, demotes other in-progress tasks, syncs reverse blocked_by/blocks, all in one function. The dependency-validation logic also does linear scans over self.tasks for every reference (any(|t| t.id == bid)) — O(N) per dependency on a Vec lookup that should be O(1). Cycle detection allocates a HashSet+Vec stack every call. Combined: O(M*N) for M dependencies in a task with N total tasks.

The single-in-progress invariant enforcement is intertwined with status-string parsing and inline 'task removed' early-return that yields Ok(None) — overloads the return.
## Mandated refactor
Replace Vec<Task> with BTreeMap<String, Task> or HashMap for O(1) lookup. Split update_task into update_status, update_metadata, add_dependency. Return typed UpdateOutcome { Updated, Deleted } instead of Ok(Option<&Task>). Hoist would_create_cycle into a struct that maintains a reverse-edge index for O(1) cycle checks on adjacent edges.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
